trials.justification: scoring parse error: claude scoring exited non-zero:

Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only (task_context states 'Sprint 1 webhook delivery only'). Sprint 2 retry logic is explicitly excluded with direct instruction ('do not implement it'). All three ACs are present (POST with JSON, 200 OK delivered, 503 failed). 5-second timeout is included in domain_rules and architecture_guidance. MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed to Shaq as a design note in both task_context and architecture_guidance. No retry logic appears in implementation guidance.

observations.text: Task context leads with explicit Sprint 1 scoping and Sprint 2 exclusion in a single clear sentence
Extension points guidance preserved faithfully — explains returning DeliveryResult rather than void as the minimal extension point
5-second timeout appears in domain_rules, architecture_guidance, and terminology_alignment
All three ACs present with Given/When/Then format
Architecture guidance explicitly says 'No retry, no scheduling, no state machine'
Open questions show good judgment — flags event payload type ambiguity and network failure edge case
AC3 covers 503 specifically but does not explicitly test the timeout failure case (5s exceeded), though the domain_rules do cover it

trials.justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only (task_context states 'Sprint 2 is explicitly deferred — do not implement it'). Sprint 2 retry logic is acknowledged and explicitly excluded with direct instruction to Shaq. All three original ACs are present (AC1: successful 2xx delivery, AC2: non-2xx failure, AC3: JSON POST payload), plus a bonus AC4 for any-2xx. 5-second timeout is included in domain rules and architecture guidance. MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed through as a dedicated architecture_guidance entry. No retry logic appears in implementation guidance — architecture explicitly says 'No queue infrastructure, no retry loops, no backoff logic.'

observations.text: Sprint 1 scoping is explicit in task_context and reinforced throughout architecture_guidance
Dedicated architecture entry for 'Do not implement WebhookRetryScheduler or any BullMQ integration' with clear rationale
Extension points guidance faithfully relayed: 'Leave natural extension points for retry without implementing them'
All three required ACs present plus a fourth covering any-2xx edge case
5-second timeout appears in domain rules, architecture guidance, and acceptance criteria
Terminology alignment section adds clarity for implementer
Open questions are well-chosen and practical (HTTP client, error handling contract, sentinel values)
AC3 tests JSON payload but does not explicitly test the failure/timeout case (5s timeout as AC); timeout is covered in domain rules but not as a standalone AC
